_comment: WIN-SYS-007 certificates / digital signatures / macro security. This is a composition-PINNING contract that proves the HARD CONSTRAINT: each of the five owned cert/signature/macro-security dialog roots stays MODAL (native-exclusion) and is never routed to the bottom-right notification stack. It binds four independent evidence layers per dialog: (1) the shared dialog-notification-policy.csv keeps the root policy=native-exclusion with the matching exclusion reason; (2) the shared sfx2 NotificationRouter classifier -- imported LIVE from bin/check-windows-dialog-notification-contract.py, not reimplemented -- returns the native exclusion for the dialog, the 'security' reason for the four xmlsec roots and the 'input' reason for cui's CertDialog, pinning the router's own security-vs-input precedence; (3) each .ui modal footer action-widget mapping matches the pinned Help|Close/OK/Cancel set; and (4) comment-stripped source reachability that every root is a synchronous modal GenericDialogController bound to its pinned .ui and executed via GenericDialogController::run(). ui_file is the repository path (for CSV/.ui lookup); the source binds the 'xmlsec/ui' / 'cui/ui' aliases, kept in the modal_markers, so the two are distinct fields. SecurityOptionsDialog, signatureline and signsignatureline are also security-classified but belong to the Tools>Options security page and signature-insertion flows (adjacent rows), so they are deliberately excluded here to avoid double ownership. No native build, dialog pixels or Material redraw of any security control is claimed; runtime_verified stays false.

modal_markers.note: synchronous modal controller bound to the pinned .ui
executed via the base synchronous run()
synchronous modal controller bound to the pinned .ui

dialogs.classification_note: the 'security' router reason for this root derives from the 'xmlsecurity' path substring plus the 'certificat' object-id token; a refactor moving it out of an xmlsecurity/ path would drop it to 'input' and fail this contract (correct fail-closed behaviour).

executed via the base synchronous run()

dialogs.classification_note: the 'security' router reason for this root derives from the 'xmlsecurity' path substring (its object id alone carries no security token); a refactor out of an xmlsecurity/ path would drop it to 'input' and fail this contract (correct fail-closed behaviour).

modal_markers.note: synchronous modal controller bound to the pinned .ui

dialogs.classification_note: certpath's CertDialog stays modal through the router's 'input' precedence (a certificate-path TreeView), not the security token -- an honest distinction from the four xmlsec roots.
